channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: June’s strawberry moon is peaking soon. Here’s what to know - CNN
item.description:
1. June’s strawberry moon is peaking soon. Here’s what to know  CNN
  2. Look up Monday evening for the full strawberry moon  WRAL
  3. The ‘strawberry micromoon' is set to rise over the Chicago area Monday  NBC 5 Chicago
  4. This Week's Sky at a Glance, June 26 – July 5  Sky & Telescope
  5. June's Strawberry Moon is unlike any other full moon. Here's why  Space

item.title: NASA Aims to Catch a Falling Space Telescope and Push It Back Up - The New York Times
item.description:
1. NASA Aims to Catch a Falling Space Telescope and Push It Back Up  The New York Times
  2. NASA prepares to launch daring rescue to save aging telescope from falling to Earth  CBS News
  3. Inside NASA’s daring race to save a doomed space telescope  USA Today
  4. NASA prepares to launch an unprecedented mission to save a dying space telescope  Scientific American
  5. NASA to launch rescue mission June 30 to save Swift space telescope from burning up in Earth's atmosphere  Space

item.title: 'The fate of Earth depends on a delicate balance': Our planet may survive the death of the sun after all, new models hint - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'The fate of Earth depends on a delicate balance': Our planet may survive the death of the sun after all, new models hint  Live Science
  2. Will Earth survive the red giant sun? New research suggests yes  EarthSky
  3. Earth Could Survive the Sun’s Bloated Death Stage, But There’s a Catch  Gizmodo
  4. ☀️ Will Earth survive the end of the Sun?  Techno-Science.net
  5. Earth Might Not Be Engulfed By The Sun In 5 Billion Years After All  IFLScience

item.title: China Is About To Land On Earth’s ‘Mini-Moon’ — What To Know - Forbes
item.description:
1. China Is About To Land On Earth’s ‘Mini-Moon’ — What To Know  Forbes
  2. China’s Tianwen-2 mission has (probably) arrived at a quasi-moon of Earth  The Planetary Society
  3. Humanity has spent well over a billion dollars bringing asteroid pieces back to Earth — and all the material returned by Hayabusa, Hayabusa2, and OSIRIS-REx combined weighs about 127 grams, less than a small apple.  Space Daily
  4. A radio amateur decodes the signal from China's Tianwen-2 probe on its way to an asteroid  Korben
  5. How Asteroid Sample Return Went From a Long Shot to a Four-Nation Race  KeepTrack

item.title: First ever dinosaur fossil discovered on Antarctica identified as a titanosaur - Natural History Museum
item.description:
1. First ever dinosaur fossil discovered on Antarctica identified as a titanosaur  Natural History Museum

  3. This Fossil Sat in Storage for 40 Years Before Scientists Realized It Was Antarctica’s First Dinosaur Bone  ZME Science
  4. First Dinosaur Fossil Ever Found In Antarctica Is From A Titanosaur, The Largest Dinosaurs To Walk On Earth  IFLScience
  5. First ever dinosaur found in Antarctica described for science  Phys.org

item.title: Physicists create a strange new quantum state called a fractional fermi sea - ScienceDaily
item.description:
1. Physicists create a strange new quantum state called a fractional fermi sea  ScienceDaily

  4. Physicists Set Sail on a Strange Quantum State Called a Fractional Fermi Sea  The Quantum Insider
  5. "It Has a Hidden Order That Becomes Visible”: Physicists Just Discovered a Bizarre New Quantum State  The Debrief

item.title: Two ‘Super-Puff’ Planets Lighter Than Candy Candy Discovered by Researchers - Good News Network
item.description:
1. Two ‘Super-Puff’ Planets Lighter Than Candy Candy Discovered by Researchers  Good News Network
  2. NASA’s TESS Mission Reveals the “Puffiest” Planets Ever Found  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. Astronomers find biggest super-puff planets yet, lighter than cotton candy  Los Angeles Times
  4. Two Planets Lighter Than Candy Floss  Universe Today
  5. Astronomers found two rare super puff planets lighter than cotton candy  ScienceDaily

item.title: In July 2025, the ATLAS sky survey in Chile spotted a faint comet racing through the solar system at roughly 130,000 miles per hour — and follow-up orbit calculations quickly showed it was not bound to the Sun at all, but had arrived from interstellar space. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In July 2025, the ATLAS sky survey in Chile spotted a faint comet racing through the solar system at roughly 130,000 miles per hour — and follow-up orbit calculations quickly showed it was not bound to the Sun at all, but had arrived from interstellar space.  Space Daily
  2. A Rare Comet Made History as the Third Known Interstellar Object to Fly Through Our Solar System. Studies Are Now Revealing the Mysterious Conditions in Which It Formed  Smithsonian Magazine
  3. Interstellar comet that zoomed past Earth could be oldest and coldest object ever seen in solar system, astronomers say  CBS News
  4. 12 billion years old, this interstellar comet is older than our solar system  Phys.org
  5. Deuterium in Comets Tells Interesting Tales  Universe Today

item.title: The James Webb telescope picked out 16.5 million individual stars in the Cigar Galaxy — a neighbor forming stars ten times faster than the Milky Way, in a burst estimated to last only a few hundred million years - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The James Webb telescope picked out 16.5 million individual stars in the Cigar Galaxy — a neighbor forming stars ten times faster than the Milky Way, in a burst estimated to last only a few hundred million years  Space Daily
  2. NASA’s Webb Pinpoints Millions of Stars Within Cigar Galaxy  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. James Webb Space Telescope Captures the Cigar Galaxy’s Brilliant Stellar Halo in Pristine Detail  Nautilus | Science
  4. In a New 223-Megapixel Image, NASA Captures 16.5 Million Stars of the Cigar Galaxy  Colossal
  5. The Galaxy Living Too Fast  Universe Today

item.title: Artifacts dating back 400,000 years, found in cave, show ‘complex and rich’ pre-human society - CNN
item.description:
1. Artifacts dating back 400,000 years, found in cave, show ‘complex and rich’ pre-human society  CNN
  2. A newly revealed cave in Israel may reveal secrets of pre-Neanderthals  Haaretz
  3. A 780,000-year-old tool secret is changing what we know about early humans  The Economic Times
  4. A mysterious cave collapse reveals a 300,000-year-old "advanced human time capsule"  vocal.media
  5. Archaeologists uncover 400,000-year-old cave used by pre-Neanderthal hominins near Fureidis  Межа. Новини України.

item.title: The James Webb Space Telescope peered into one of the universe's oldest galaxy clusters, and scientists can't explain what they saw - Space
item.description:
1. The James Webb Space Telescope peered into one of the universe's oldest galaxy clusters, and scientists can't explain what they saw  Space
  2. The JWST Spies Six Galaxies Becoming One  Universe Today
  3. The James Webb Space Telescope found a spiral galaxy, nicknamed the Big Wheel, that existed just two billion years after the Big Bang — five times more massive than the Milky Way, with a spiral structure our models say couldn't have survived that early  Space Daily
  4. Webb Telescope Finds Giant Galaxy, Black Hole Birth  Mirage News
  5. James Webb Space Telescope Spots Six Galaxies Merging Into One 12 Billion Years Ago  Tech Times

item.title: In 2020, something near the centre of the Milky Way switched on and off six times in radio waves, then disappeared from every follow-up search in X-rays and infrared — leaving astronomers with only the name ASKAP J173608.2−321635 and the possibility of - Space Daily
item.description: In 2020, something near the centre of the Milky Way switched on and off six times in radio waves, then disappeared from every follow-up search in X-rays and infrared — leaving astronomers with only the name ASKAP J173608.2−321635 and the possibility of  Space Daily

item.title: China was effectively shut out of the International Space Station after the United States passed the Wolf Amendment in 2011, so it built its own. If the ISS retires on schedule around 2030 and no commercial successor is ready, China's Tiangong could become t - Space Daily
item.description:
1. China was effectively shut out of the International Space Station after the United States passed the Wolf Amendment in 2011, so it built its own. If the ISS retires on schedule around 2030 and no commercial successor is ready, China's Tiangong could become t  Space Daily

  3. China’s Space Station Set to Expand  Inside Outer Space

item.title: We assumed humpback whales had to reach warm tropical waters before giving birth, but a 2025 UNSW-led study found newborn calves as far south as Tasmania — around 1,500 kilometres beyond the accepted calving zone — suggesting some mothers giv - Space Daily

item.title: Sister Mary, a Catholic nun who belonged to the School Sisters of Notre Dame and died at 101 with her memory and reasoning fully intact, was one of 678 nuns enrolled in a 30-year study of brain aging by the American researcher David Snowdon — and her - Space Daily
